title: [FORMATTING]
text: [FORMATTING] The [FORMATTING] section of the assignment file allows you to assign rules and customize the code formatting tool in the Source Editor. Syntax [FORMATTING] Rule { = } Value Arguments Rule: The rule specification syntax. Refer to the following Code Formatting Rules table for the list of all possible options. Value: The value for the specified rule syntax. Example: Setting Rules The following example of the assignment file defines a rule that turns off the variable alignment feature when formatting the ProcScript code: Explain this code [FORMATTING] alignVariables = false Copy Description Uniface provides various rules for customizing the behavior of the code formatting tool in the Uniface IDE ProcScript Editors. Table 1. Code Formatting Rules Rule Data Type Description Possible Values Default Value AlignVariables boolean Align the contents of 'variables' blocks into perfect columns. The columns may contain in the following order: Datatype and type-specific attributes, Parameter name, Direction, and Comment (optional). true, false true AlignParams boolean Align the contents of 'params' blocks into perfect columns. The columns may contain in the following order: Datatype and type-specific attributes, Variable name, and Comment (optional). true, false true IndentVariables boolean Add extra indentation to the 'variables' block. By default, the block's indentation starts from zero. This is only effective when the block is placed into a trigger, operation, function, etc. Component variables are not affected. true, false false IndentParams boolean Add extra indentation to the 'params' block. By default, the block's indentation starts from zero. true, false false IndentScope boolean Add extra indentation to the 'scope' block. By default, the block's indentation starts from zero. true, false false IndentTabs boolean When true, indentation is performed with tabs. When false, indentation is performed with spaces. true, false true IndentSize integer When using tabs, IndentSize determines the width of a tab. When using spaces, IndentSize determines the number of spaces that represent a single tab. 1, 2, 3, 4, ... 4 Note: Boolean types accept all Uniface boolean values, such as 0, 1, true, and false. Example: Modifying the IndentParams rule You need to first activate the rule in your ASN file as shown below: Explain this code [FORMATTING] IndentParams = false Copy Behavior on the code formatting tool Formatted code with the rule disabled: Explain this code ; Before enabling the rule - Default behavior. operation myOper params string myString : OUT endparams myString = "Operation complete." end Copy Formatted code with the rule enabled: Explain this code ; After enabling the rule - Customized behavior. operation myOper params string myString : INOUT endparams myString = "Operation complete." end Copy Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
